title: podman pull command does not give status message when Image is up to date
body:
### Issue Description

Unlike docker, podman pull command does not give status message when Image is up to date.
See below output from docker pull command. Status message is missing in podman pull.

latest-csi-amd64: Pulling from <IMAGE>
Digest: sha256:<sha>
Status: Image is up to date for <IMAGE>

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Are you scripting for this or just want it because it makes for a better human experience?
it is a nth I will admit, but podman does not need to match the docker experience bit by bit.  as @rhatdan asks, is there a hard requirement for it?
@rhatdan I’m currently using it in the script. While it works fine for Docker, unfortunately, it doesn’t function the same with Podman. Having a status at the end would be a nice feature.
what @rhatdan and i are wondering is if you depend on that exact message because you are looking for a specific string sequence or if, like you said, it would be a nice feature.
You really should rely on the exit code, of success or failure.

Well the exit code is zero in this case. I've already tried that. See below:

[root@c54415v1 ~]# podman pull docker.io/clamav/clamav:latest
Trying to pull docker.io/clamav/clamav:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a6a68ddc37b8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 635391e20f50 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 2bc4ebb26169 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 65dd3aaf10a8 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 74b89fe6c8d9 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 57febbfb5458 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 4abcf2066143 skipped: already exists
Copying config c9f71cb597 done
Writing manifest to image destination
c9f71cb59795ba6a22385dd431cc8270a8215a1e50326fa293ce6895288ee08d
[root@c54415v1 ~]# echo $?
0
So you need to differentiate between whether it pulled an image or image pre-existed?

@rhatdan Yes that is correct. The docker provides that status perfectly.
**Status: Image is up to date for ....**

```
# podman pull docker.io/clamav/clamav:latest 2>/dev/null
c9f71cb59795ba6a22385dd431cc8270a8215a1e50326fa293ce6895288ee08d
# docker pull docker.io/clamav/clamav:latest 2>/dev/null
latest: Pulling from clamav/clamav
Digest: sha256:0bb5d72ed47458c3333868c2e5835dd38f4268ceeba6e4212b3c57d809526755
Status: Image is up to date for clamav/clamav:latest
docker.io/clamav/clamav:latest
```
Our mechanism is better for scripting where as Docker is better for Humans, we could print similar data to Docker but it would have to go to STDERR not STDOUT, since it would be a big breaking change to Podman for questionable value.

Even crictl give the status of the pull image: 
root@c51:~/# crictl pull clamav/clamav:latest
Image is up to date for sha256:c9f71cb59795ba6a22385dd431cc8270a8215a1e50326fa293ce6895288ee08d
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
